workflow.name: Send appointment SMS follow-ups with Typeform, Twilio and Google Sheets
workflow.description:
## How it works

- 📋 Customer submits your Typeform → instant personalised welcome SMS fires via Twilio
- 📊 Submission logged to Google Sheets with name, phone, timestamp, and nudge tracking
- 💬 Twilio listens 24/7 for replies → marks customer as responded, stops all future nudges
- ⏰ Scheduler runs every 6 hours → finds non-responders and sends up to 2 follow-up nudges
- 🛑 System stops contacting anyone who replies or hits the 2-nudge limit

## The three branches

| Branch | Trigger | What it does |
|--------|---------|--------------|
| 🟢 New Submission | Typeform form submit | Welcome SMS + log to Sheets |
| 🔵 Reply Handler | Incoming Twilio SMS | Mark replied, stop nudges |
| 🟡 Nudge Engine | Schedule every 6hrs | Nudge 1 at 24h, Nudge 2 at 48h |

## Why this matters

| Metric | Manual | This workflow |
|--------|--------|---------------|
| ⚡ Speed to lead | Hours or days | Under 10 seconds |
| 🔁 Follow-up consistency | Whoever remembers | 100% automated |
| 📉 Lead drop-off | High — people go cold fast | Nudges catch non-responders |
| 🧠 Staff time per lead | 5–15 min | Zero |
| 💸 Revenue at risk | Every ignored inquiry | Recovered automatically |

Studies show leads contacted within 5 minutes are 9x more likely to convert. This workflow makes that the default — not the exception.

## What you need

| Tool | Cost | Why |
|------|------|-----|
| Typeform | Free tier works | Intake/booking form |
| Twilio | ~$1/mo + $0.0075/SMS | Send and receive SMS |
| Google Sheets | Free | Track submissions and nudge state |

⚠️ **A2P 10DLC notice:** Twilio requires business registration for production SMS in the US. Allow 2-4 weeks. Your signup form needs opt-in language, terms, and a privacy policy.

## Set up steps

- Takes about 15 minutes
- Create a Google Sheet with columns: customerName · customerPhone · submittedAt · repliedAt · nudgeCount · lastNudgeAt
- Branch 1 — connect Typeform, select your form, connect Twilio + Google Sheets
- Branch 2 — connect Twilio trigger for inbound SMS, connect Google Sheets to mark replied
- Branch 3 — connect Google Sheets to read non-responders, connect Twilio to send nudges
- Verify Typeform field names match the Extract node exactly (case-sensitive)
- Submit a test entry with your own phone, reply to it, confirm Sheets updates correctly
- Toggle workflow Active


### More where this came from →[Orchestrate Academy](https://orchestrate.academy)

parameters.content:
## 📖 README: Automated SMS Follow-Up System

**What This Does**
A complete 3-branch system that sends a welcome SMS when someone submits your Typeform, then automatically follows up with up to 2 nudge messages if they don't reply — all while respecting responses and stopping nudges the moment someone engages.

**The Three Branches:**

**Branch 1: New Submission Flow** (Typeform Trigger)
- Instant welcome SMS when form is submitted
- Logs customer data to Google Sheets tracking table
- Starts the customer journey

**Branch 2: SMS Reply Handler** (Twilio Trigger)
- Listens 24/7 for incoming SMS replies
- Marks customer as "replied" in Google Sheets
- Stops all future nudges for that customer

**Branch 3: Automated Nudge System** (Schedule Trigger - every 6 hours)
- Checks for non-responders who need follow-up
- Sends Nudge 1 at 24 hours after welcome SMS
- Sends Nudge 2 at 24 hours after Nudge 1
- Stops after 2 nudges (respects customer boundaries)

**Who This Is For**
- Dental & medical practices — welcome new patients and ensure they confirm appointments
- Fitness studios — greet new members and get them to book their first session
- Law firms — acknowledge inquiries and prompt for next steps
- Real estate agents — confirm receipt and encourage follow-up questions
- Agencies or local businesses - follow up with leads instantly
- Any service business using Typeform for bookings/intake

**What You Need**
- Typeform account (free tier works)
- Twilio account + phone number (~$1/mo for number, ~$0.0075/SMS)
**!! WARNING !! getting A2P verification for a 10DLC can be challenging and take weeks**
*A2P registration is highly regulated and requires careful opt in disclosures on signup form, term, and privacy policy will be reviewed for compliance without exception*
- Google Sheets (free)
- Your form must collect Name and Phone Number fields

**Setup Steps**

1. **Google Sheets Setup**
   - Create a new spreadsheet for tracking
   - Add headers: customerName | customerPhone | submittedAt | repliedAt | nudgeCount | lastNudgeAt

2. **Branch 1: New Submission Flow**
   - Connect Typeform credential on "New Form Submission" trigger
   - Select your form from dropdown
   - Verify field names in "Extract Name and Phone" match your form exactly
   - Connect Twilio credential on "Send Welcome SMS"
   - Set your Twilio From number (+1XXXXXXXXXX)
   - Connect Google Sheets credential on "Track Submissions"
   - Select your tracking spreadsheet and sheet

3. **Branch 2: SMS Reply Handler**
   - Connect Twilio credential on "Incoming SMS Reply" trigger
   - Connect Google Sheets credential on "Mark as Replied"
   - Select same tracking spreadsheet

4. **Branch 3: Automated Nudge System**
   - Connect Google Sheets credential on "Get Non-Responders"
   - Select same tracking spreadsheet
   - Connect Twilio credential on "Send Nudge SMS"
   - Set your Twilio From number (same as Branch 1)
   - Connect Google Sheets credential on "Update Nudge Count"

5. **Test & Activate**
   - Submit a test form with your own phone
   - Confirm welcome SMS arrives
   - Reply to the SMS and check that Google Sheets updates
   - Toggle workflow Active

**How It Works Together**

1. Customer submits form → Welcome SMS sent + tracked in Sheets
2. If customer replies → Sheets updated, nudges stop
3. If no reply after 24 hours → Nudge 1 sent
4. If still no reply after another 24 hours → Nudge 2 sent
5. After 2 nudges or any reply → System stops contacting that customer

**Troubleshooting**
- SMS not sending → Check Twilio credentials, confirm From number is active
- Nudges not sending → Verify Google Sheets has data, check "Get Non-Responders" filters
- Reply not stopping nudges → Confirm "Mark as Replied" is updating the correct sheet
- Schedule not running → Make sure workflow is Active

---

## Learn more at [Orchestrate Academy](https://www.orchestrate.academy)

parameters.content:
## 🎯 Branch 1: New Submission Flow

**Trigger:** Typeform submission

**What It Does:**
1. Captures new form submissions instantly
2. Extracts customer name and phone number
3. Sends personalized welcome SMS via Twilio
4. Logs submission to Google Sheets tracking table

**Data Tracked:**
- Customer name & phone
- Submission timestamp
- Reply status (empty initially)
- Nudge count (starts at 0)
- Last nudge timestamp (empty initially)

**Key Point:** This is the entry point that kicks off the entire customer journey.

## 💬 Branch 3: SMS Reply Handler

**Trigger:** Incoming SMS to your Twilio number

**What It Does:**
1. Listens for customer SMS replies 24/7
2. Captures reply timestamp
3. Updates Google Sheets to mark customer as "replied"
4. **Stops all future nudges** for that customer

**Why This Matters:**
The moment a customer replies, they're engaged. No need to nudge them anymore. This branch ensures you respect their response and don't over-communicate.

**Key Point:** This is your "kill switch" for the nudge sequence — one reply stops everything.

## ⏰ Branch 2: Automated Nudge System

**Trigger:** Every 6 hours (Schedule)

**What It Does:**
1. Queries Google Sheets for non-responders
2. Filters customers who haven't replied AND haven't hit max nudges (2)
3. Checks if enough time has passed (24 hours since last contact)
4. Sends follow-up SMS via Twilio
5. Updates nudge count and timestamp in Google Sheets

**Nudge Schedule:**
- Nudge 1: 24 hours after welcome SMS
- Nudge 2: 24 hours after Nudge 1
- Then stops (max 2 nudges)

**Key Point:** Runs every 6 hours to catch customers at the right time, but only sends when conditions are met.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
